A rectangle has side lengths in the ratio of $2:5$. If all of its sides are extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the resulting rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $3:7$. 

*highlighted section* The original ratio of the sides is $2:5$, which means if the shorter side is $2x$, the longer side is $5x$. When extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths become $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$. The new ratio is given as $3:7$, so we can set up the equation:

$$
\frac{2x + 9}{5x + 9} = \frac{3}{7}
$$

*highlighted section* Cross-multiplying gives:

$$
7(2x + 9) = 3(5x + 9)
$$

$$
14x + 63 = 15x + 27
$$

$$
63 - 27 = 15x - 14x
$$

$$
36 = x
$$

So, the original side lengths are $2x = 12$ cm and $5x = 60$ cm.

*highlighted section* Now, if we extend all of its sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths will be:

$$
12 + 9 = 21 \text{ cm} \quad \text{and} \quad 60 + 9 = 69 \text{ cm}
$$

*highlighted section* The new ratio of the side lengths is $21:69$, which can be simplified by dividing both numbers by their greatest common divisor, which is $3$:

$$
\frac{21}{69} = \frac{7}{23}
$$

*highlighted section* Therefore, the final ratio of the side lengths after extending all sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$ is $7:23$.
